mapping.a_element: decay regimes (algebraic/exponential) of reactant lifetime distribution in phase space
mapping.b_element: chaotic vs. stable dynamical regimes in the solar system due to resonance overlap

mapping.a_element: statistical estimates of isomerization rate (transition state theory)
mapping.b_element: analytic theory estimating Lyapunov time and dynamical lifetime

mapping.a_element: symmetry-related product channels in phase space
mapping.b_element: mean motion resonance components among Jovian planets

mapping.a_element: short timescale (t ≲ 0.5 ps) for symmetry attainment in trajectory ensemble
mapping.b_element: timescale for chaos onset due to resonance overlap

brief_justification: While both papers analyze dynamical systems with distinct regimes (decay vs. chaos) and use statistical or analytic estimates, paper A focuses on phase space transition state theory and gap time distributions for chemical reactions, whereas paper B examines resonance overlap and Lyapunov exponents in celestial mechanics. The underlying machinery—chemical kinetics vs. orbital dynamics—differs fundamentally.
